title: Wet-Dry Compound Transition Pressure
summary: Wet-weather tyre changes are driven by track-condition transitions and are not governed by the same mandatory dry-compound logic.
rationale: Wet-weather tyre changes are driven by track-condition transitions and are not governed by the same mandatory dry-compound logic. Current wet flags are static; the stronger signal may be whether the field is collectively switching between slicks, intermediates, and wets.
implementation_hint: For each Race_Year and lap, compute slick/intermediate/wet field shares and share deltas versus prior lap. Add driver-on-minority-compound, compound-mismatch-to-field-majority, slick_to_wet_pressure, wet_to_slick_pressure, and interactions with RaceProgress and Position.
expected_effect: Should improve rare but high-signal weather-transition stops, where many drivers pit within one or two laps.
risk: Wet rows may be sparse, and using next-lap compound mix would leak; keep features current/prior-lap only.
